growth_analysis.details: Revenue data incomplete; Flat or declining FCFF

risk_analysis.details: Beta NA; D/E 0.0; Interest coverage NA

macro_context.market_sentiment: Bullish: April 2026 was the S&P 500's best month since 2020, with indices closing at new all-time highs. Futures edged higher on May 1 though Q1 GDP came in at 2.0% vs 2.3% expected, signaling slightly softer growth. PCE inflation running at 3.5%, above the Fed's 2% target, creating a mixed macro backdrop.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026 in an unusually divided 8-4 vote. Jerome Powell's tenure as chair is ending; Kevin Warsh's nomination advanced in Senate. Rate cuts remain on hold given persistent inflation from tariffs and energy prices. No near-term cut expected.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East conflict (Strait of Hormuz risk) has pushed energy prices sharply higher; U.S. tariff regime persists, complicating Fed policy and corporate cost outlooks. U.S. posturing in Venezuela and NATO/Greenland tensions add uncertainty. IMF downgraded global growth to 3.1%. Semiconductor supply chains face additional scrutiny under trade policy.
key_events: AMD Q1 2026 earnings: May 5, 2026 after market close
Kevin Warsh Senate confirmation vote for Fed chair (pending)
Ongoing FOMC rate path evaluation with PCE at 3.5%
Middle East energy supply risks (Strait of Hormuz)

ticker_news.headline: AMD hits all-time high of $353, up 246% over the past year, driven by AI chip demand and analyst upgrades ahead of earnings
DA Davidson upgrades AMD to Buy, raises price target from $220 to $375; also lifts 2026 revenue estimate by $2B above Wall Street consensus
Susquehanna reiterates Buy on AMD, raises price target from $300 to $375 citing data center GPU demand ahead of earnings
AMD Q1 2026 earnings scheduled for May 5 after market close; stock has rallied 63% YTD into the print
Northland Capital Markets downgrades AMD to Market Perform with $260 price target, citing competitive concerns in semiconductor landscape

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Predominantly bullish: DA Davidson upgraded to Buy with PT $375 (from $220), Susquehanna raised PT to $375 (from $300), Envision Research upgraded to Buy on agentic AI positioning. Counterpoint: Northland Capital Markets downgraded to Market Perform with $260 PT on April 27. Overall consensus: 34 analysts covering AMD — 41% Strong Buy, 38% Buy, 21% Hold, 0% Sell. PT range $120-$375.

web_context.competitor_activity: Intel reported Q1 2026 revenue of $13.58B (+7% YoY) with data center/AI revenue up 22% to $5.05B — a positive read-through for AMD's competing EPYC server CPU franchise. NVIDIA's data center revenue exceeded $30B in Q1 2026 with Blackwell GPU dominance, though it faces a 40% production cut from memory shortages. AMD is positioned as the preferred cost-efficient second supplier for hyperscalers, with a 6-gigawatt OpenAI deployment commitment and 50,000 MI450 GPU order from Oracle by Q3 2026. The x86 CPU market shows a renaissance benefiting AMD in the PC and server segments.
web_context.earnings_info: AMD reports Q1 2026 fiscal results on May 5, 2026 after market close (5:00 PM ET / 2:00 PM PT). Analyst EPS consensus estimate: ~$1.24. Stock has rallied sharply into the print; investors watching for data center GPU (MI-series) revenue ramp and EPYC CPU market share gains. Any guidance raise or AI customer pipeline disclosure could be a significant catalyst.
